description: x86-64 (AMD64 / Intel 64) instruction additions and extensions. Covers instructions that are new in 64-bit long mode, instructions whose encodings change with the REX.W prefix for 64-bit operand size, quad-word string operation variants, the SYSCALL/SYSRET system-call interface, and instructions removed in 64-bit mode. The REX prefix byte (0x40–0x4F) selects 64-bit operand size (REX.W = bit 3 set = 0x48) and encodes access to R8–R15 and XMM8–XMM15.

notes.rex_prefix: REX prefix byte range: 0x40–0x4F. REX.W (0x48) selects 64-bit operand size. REX.R/X/B extend the register fields of the ModRM and SIB bytes to access R8–R15.
notes.default_operand_size: In 64-bit mode the default operand size is 32 bits; REX.W is needed to select 64-bit. PUSH/POP default to 64 bits without REX.
notes.removed_instructions: DAA, DAS, AAA, AAS, AAM, AAD, BOUND, INTO, LDS, LES, PUSHA/POPA are removed in 64-bit long mode.

opcodes.description: Sign-extend 32-bit r/m32 into 64-bit register (REX.W + opcode 0x63)

instructions.description: Sign-extend a 32-bit register or memory operand into a 64-bit register. Distinct from MOVSX (which operates on 8/16-bit sources). Opcode 0x63 was ARPL in 32-bit mode.
instructions.notes: REX.W prefix (0x48) is required to distinguish from ARPL (0x63 in 32-bit mode).

opcodes.description: Push 64-bit RFLAGS register onto stack (default operand size is 64 bits in long mode)

instructions.description: Push the 64-bit RFLAGS register onto the stack. In 64-bit mode opcode 0x9C pushes RFLAGS (64-bit) by default.
instructions.notes: Same opcode 0x9C as PUSHF/PUSHFD; the operand size is 64-bit by default in long mode.

opcodes.description: Pop 64-bit value from stack into RFLAGS (default operand size is 64 bits in long mode)

instructions.description: Pop a 64-bit value from the stack into RFLAGS. In 64-bit mode opcode 0x9D pops RFLAGS (64-bit) by default.
instructions.notes: Same opcode 0x9D as POPF/POPFD; the operand size is 64-bit by default in long mode.

opcodes.description: Sign-extend EAX into RAX (REX.W + opcode 0x98); 64-bit rename of CWDE

instructions.description: Sign-extend EAX into RAX. 64-bit rename of CWDE (which sign-extends AX into EAX in 32-bit mode). Same opcode 0x98 with REX.W prefix.

opcodes.description: Sign-extend RAX into RDX:RAX (REX.W + opcode 0x99); 64-bit rename of CDQ

instructions.description: Sign-extend RAX into RDX:RAX. 64-bit rename of CDQ (which sign-extends EAX into EDX:EAX in 32-bit mode). Used before 64-bit IDIV.

opcodes.description: Compare RDX:RAX with 128-bit memory; if equal store RCX:RBX to memory; else load memory into RDX:RAX (REX.W required)

instructions.description: Compare RDX:RAX with a 128-bit memory value; if equal, store RCX:RBX; otherwise load memory into RDX:RAX. Requires LOCK for atomicity.
instructions.notes: Requires the CMPXCHG16B feature flag (CPUID.01H:ECX.bit13=1). Use LOCK prefix for atomicity.

opcodes.description: Exchange GS base register with IA32_KERNEL_GS_BASE MSR; used in OS kernel entry/exit

instructions.description: Exchange the base of the GS register with the IA32_KERNEL_GS_BASE MSR. Used in OS kernel entry/exit to switch between user and kernel GS bases.
instructions.notes: Ring-0 only.

opcodes.description: Transfer to OS kernel: save RIP→RCX, RFLAGS→R11; load RIP from LSTAR MSR; switch to ring-0

instructions.description: Fast system-call mechanism used in 64-bit mode; transfers control to the OS kernel using the LSTAR MSR. Saves RIP into RCX and RFLAGS into R11.
instructions.notes: Preferred over INT 0x80 on x86-64. SYSCALL clobbers RCX and R11. Linux syscall ABI: rax=syscall number, rdi/rsi/rdx/r10/r8/r9=args.

opcodes.description: Return from SYSCALL: restore RIP from RCX, RFLAGS from R11; switch to ring-3 (32-bit return)
Return from SYSCALL in 64-bit mode (REX.W + 0x0F 0x07); restores full 64-bit RIP from RCX

instructions.description: Companion return instruction for SYSCALL. Restores RIP from RCX and RFLAGS from R11.
instructions.notes: Requires ring-0. Use SYSRETQ (REX.W form) to return to 64-bit user-mode code.

opcodes.description: 64-bit interrupt return: pop RIP (8), CS (8), RFLAGS (8), RSP (8), SS (8) from stack (40 bytes total)

instructions.description: 64-bit Interrupt Return: pops 8-byte RIP, CS, RFLAGS, RSP, and SS from the stack (40 bytes). REX.W prefix (0x48) selects 64-bit form.
instructions.notes: Without REX.W, 0xCF in 64-bit mode pops a 32-bit CS:EIP frame. IRETQ is the standard form for 64-bit interrupt handlers.

opcodes.description: Short jump if RCX=0 (in 64-bit mode with 64-bit address size; same opcode as JCXZ/JECXZ)

instructions.description: Jump if RCX is Zero (flag-free test). The 64-bit form of JCXZ. Shares opcode 0xE3; which register is tested depends on address-size prefix.
instructions.notes: Short jump only. Address-size prefix 0x67 selects JECXZ (test ECX) instead of JRCXZ (test RCX).

opcodes.description: Copy qword from [RSI] to [RDI]; advance RSI and RDI by 8 (direction determined by DF)

instructions.description: Copy qword from [RSI] to [RDI]; advance RSI and RDI by 8 (direction flag controls sign). 64-bit quad variant of MOVSB/MOVSW/MOVSD.
instructions.notes: REX.W (0x48) + opcode 0xA5. Use with REP prefix (0xF3 0x48 0xA5) for REP MOVSQ.

opcodes.description: Compare qword at [RSI] with [RDI]; update flags; advance RSI and RDI by 8

instructions.description: Compare qword at [RSI] with [RDI]; update flags; advance both pointers. 64-bit quad variant of CMPSB/CMPSW/CMPSD.
instructions.notes: REX.W (0x48) + opcode 0xA7. Use with REPE/REPNE for REPE CMPSQ / REPNE CMPSQ.

opcodes.description: Compare RAX with qword at [RDI]; update flags; advance RDI by 8

instructions.description: Compare RAX with qword at [RDI]; update flags; advance RDI. 64-bit quad variant of SCASB/SCASW/SCASD.
instructions.notes: REX.W (0x48) + opcode 0xAF. Use with REPE/REPNE for searches.

opcodes.description: Load qword from [RSI] into RAX; advance RSI by 8

instructions.description: Load qword from [RSI] into RAX; advance RSI. 64-bit quad variant of LODSB/LODSW/LODSD.
instructions.notes: REX.W (0x48) + opcode 0xAD.

opcodes.description: Store RAX into qword at [RDI]; advance RDI by 8

instructions.description: Store RAX to qword at [RDI]; advance RDI. 64-bit quad variant of STOSB/STOSW/STOSD.
instructions.notes: REX.W (0x48) + opcode 0xAB. Use with REP prefix (0xF3 0x48 0xAB) for REP STOSQ to fill memory blocks.

opcodes.description: Count number of set bits (population count) in src; store result in dst

instructions.description: Count the number of set bits (population count) in src; store result in dst.
instructions.notes: Requires POPCNT feature flag (CPUID.01H:ECX.bit23=1). Prefix 0xF3 is a mandatory encoding prefix, not REP.

opcodes.description: Count leading zero bits in src; store result in dst; if src=0, result = operand size (e.g., 64 for 64-bit)

instructions.description: Count leading zero bits in src; store in dst. If src = 0, result is the operand size (64 for 64-bit operands).
instructions.notes: Requires LZCNT feature flag (CPUID.80000001H:ECX.bit5=1). Without the flag, 0xF3 is ignored and BSR is executed instead.

opcodes.description: Count trailing zero bits in src; store result in dst; if src=0, result = operand size

instructions.description: Count trailing zero bits in src; store in dst.
instructions.notes: Requires BMI1 feature flag (CPUID.07H:EBX.bit3=1). Without the flag, BSF is executed instead.

opcodes.description: Add with carry from CF: dst += src + CF; updates CF only (not OF)

instructions.description: Add with Carry (using CF): dst = dst + src + CF. Updates CF only; does not affect OF. Designed for multi-precision addition pipelines.
instructions.notes: Requires ADX feature flag (CPUID.07H:EBX.bit19=1). Prefix 0x66 is mandatory.

opcodes.description: Add with carry from OF: dst += src + OF; updates OF only (not CF)

instructions.description: Add with Overflow (using OF): dst = dst + src + OF. Updates OF only; does not affect CF. Designed for multi-precision addition pipelines.
instructions.notes: Requires ADX feature flag (CPUID.07H:EBX.bit19=1). Prefix 0xF3 is mandatory.

opcodes.description: Unsigned multiply: RDX × r/m32 → dst_hi:dst_lo (32-bit form, no flags)
Unsigned multiply: RDX × r/m64 → dst_hi:dst_lo (64-bit form, no flags)

instructions.description: Unsigned multiply RDX × src → dst_hi:dst_lo, without modifying flags. Allows pipelining of multi-precision multiplication.
instructions.notes: Requires BMI2 feature flag (CPUID.07H:EBX.bit8=1). VEX-encoded; see Intel manual for full VEX byte encoding.

opcodes.description: Opcode 0x27 conflicts with REX prefix space; raises #UD in long mode

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. Opcode 0x27 conflicts with the REX prefix. Decimal Adjust AL after Addition.

opcodes.description: Opcode 0x2F conflicts with REX prefix space; raises #UD in long mode

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. Decimal Adjust AL after Subtraction.

opcodes.description: Opcode 0x37 conflicts with REX prefix space; raises #UD in long mode

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. ASCII Adjust AL after Addition.

opcodes.description: Opcode 0x3F conflicts with REX prefix space; raises #UD in long mode

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. ASCII Adjust AL after Subtraction.

opcodes.description: Opcode 0xD4 conflicts with REX prefix space; raises #UD in long mode

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. ASCII Adjust AX after Multiplication.

opcodes.description: Opcode 0xD5 conflicts with REX prefix space; raises #UD in long mode

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. ASCII Adjust AX before Division.

opcodes.description: Opcode 0x62 reassigned to EVEX prefix in 64-bit mode; raises #UD if used as BOUND

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. Opcode 0x62 is the EVEX escape prefix. Check array index bounds.

opcodes.description: Opcode 0xCE reassigned in 64-bit mode; raises #UD

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. Opcode 0xCE reassigned. Interrupt on Overflow (generates INT 4 if OF=1).

opcodes.description: Opcode 0xC5 reassigned to VEX 3-byte escape prefix in 64-bit mode

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. Opcode 0xC5 is the 2-byte VEX prefix. Load far pointer into DS:reg.

opcodes.description: Opcode 0xC4 reassigned to VEX 3-byte escape prefix in 64-bit mode

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. Opcode 0xC4 is the 3-byte VEX prefix. Load far pointer into ES:reg.

opcodes.description: Opcode 0x60 is removed in 64-bit long mode; raises #UD

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. Push all general-purpose registers.

opcodes.description: Opcode 0x61 is removed in 64-bit long mode; raises #UD

instructions.description: REMOVED in 64-bit mode. Pop all general-purpose registers.
